reasoning: The resolution criteria explicitly define a self-coup to include "scenarios in which an incumbent leader refuses to relinquish power after an election." This specific clause perfectly captures the situation in Venezuela following the July 2024 election, where Nicolás Maduro lost the election according to widely verified opposition tallies but refused to step down, subsequently inaugurating himself in January 2025. Given that this question appears to have been authored around mid-2024 (as the latest listed event is June 2024), this clause was highly likely included specifically to address the heavily anticipated Venezuelan election. Furthermore, credible media reports, human rights organizations (like HRF), and governments (such as the US State Department) have characterized Maduro's actions as a coup, autogolpe, or illegitimate seizure of power. Combined with the ~15% baseline chance of a conventional military coup occurring in Africa before the July 2025 resolution date, the probability of this resolving YES is very high.
